title: Try It Yourself - Create and Switch in One Step

content.problemStatement:
You’ve just learned that you can create and switch to a new branch using a single command. \
Now let’s put that into practice!

**Your Task:** \
You’re starting work on a new feature called **“ui-update”**. \
Follow the steps below using what you’ve learned:

**Step 1:** \
Create and switch to a new branch named `ui-update` in one single command.
```sss
git switch -c ui-update
```
- Another way : Using `git checkout`
```sss
git checkout -b ui-update
```



You’ll see: \


---

**Step 2:** \
Check your current branch to make sure you’re now on `ui-update`.
```sss
git status
```
You’ll see: \


---

**Step 3:** \
List all branches to confirm that `ui-update` was created successfully.
```sss
git branch
```

You’ll see something like: 


---
